title: Jan Samarth Portal — All Government Loan Schemes in One Place
meta_description: Jan Samarth portal 2026 — single window for 15+ government loan schemes across 125+ banks. Education, business, agriculture, housing loans. Free, no agents.

hero.one_liner: One portal to apply for PM Mudra, education loans, Startup India, PM SVANidhi, PMAY subsidy, and 10+ other government credit schemes — no middlemen, no agents

what_is_it: Jan Samarth is a single-window portal launched on 6 June 2022 by PM Narendra Modi to consolidate 15+ government credit-linked schemes across 7 loan categories. Instead of running between banks trying to figure out which scheme you qualify for, you come to jansamarth.in, browse schemes, compare lenders, and apply directly. It's free, government-run, and requires no registration to explore.

The portal solves a real problem: small business owners, farmers, and students often don't know which government scheme they qualify for. Bank branch staff frequently push regular (non-subsidized) loans instead of informing you about schemes like Mudra or PMEGP. Private agents exploit this confusion and charge 1-5% commission. Jan Samarth cuts out the middleman entirely — you get transparent information about every scheme's eligibility, loan amount, interest rate, and collateral requirements upfront.

Jan Samarth integrates with Vidyalakshmi for education loans and connects you to 125+ participating institutions including SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and regional banks. The portal covers education loans, MSME/business financing (Mudra, Stand Up India, PMEGP), agricultural infrastructure, housing subsidies (PMAY CLSS), street vendor loans (SVANidhi), skill development loans, and startup funding.

Whether you're a student seeking education finance, a farmer buying equipment, a street vendor needing working capital, or an entrepreneur launching a startup — there's likely a government scheme on Jan Samarth that gives you cheaper credit than any regular bank loan. The portal is available in Hindi and English, works on mobile, and is accessible 24/7.

eligibility.label: Who can use
eligibility.value: Any Indian citizen seeking government credit schemes — students, farmers, entrepreneurs, street vendors, artisans, women, SC/ST individuals

eligibility.label: Income limit
eligibility.value: No universal limit — each scheme has its own criteria

eligibility.label: Category
eligibility.value: General, OBC, SC, ST — some schemes have category-specific benefits (Stand Up India for SC/ST/Women)

eligibility.label: Documents needed
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, PAN, address proof, income proof, bank account. Scheme-specific: business registration, admission letter, land documents etc.

eligibility.label: Registration
eligibility.value: No registration needed to browse schemes. Apply directly through partner bank's portal

extra_sections.heading: All Schemes Available on Jan Samarth
rows: Micro and small businesses, no collateral needed
Students for higher education in India or abroad
Farmers, FPOs, cooperatives for farm infrastructure
Students enrolling in skill development courses

extra_sections.heading: Why Apply Through Jan Samarth Instead of Directly at Bank
content: When you walk into a bank asking for a loan without mentioning a specific scheme, branch officers often push standard products with higher interest rates. They may not inform you about government schemes you qualify for — because scheme-linked loans involve more paperwork for them.
Jan Samarth flips this: you browse schemes, see eligibility and interest rates upfront, and apply to the RIGHT department.
Direct application through Jan Samarth bypasses agents and touts who charge 1-5% commission on your loan amount. On a ₹5 lakh Mudra loan, that's ₹5,000-₹25,000 in unnecessary fees.
The portal is 100% free — the government runs it, not a private company.
The portal shows comparative information across 125+ lenders — interest rates, processing times, required documents, and collateral requirements. You walk into the bank fully informed.
Applications are processed faster because you already have scheme details and supporting documents ready.

Many people don't even know schemes like PM SVANidhi (street vendor loans at 0% initial interest) or Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (subsidized farm equipment loans) exist. Jan Samarth centralizes all 15+ schemes in Hindi and English, accessible 24/7 from your phone.

extra_sections.heading: Loan Categories — Interest Rates and Collateral
rows: First charge on assets; may need collateral for larger loans
No collateral up to ₹4L; varies for higher amounts

extra_sections.heading: What Happens After You Apply
content: You select a scheme and lender on Jan Samarth, click 'Apply', and get redirected to the partner bank's portal. Fill out the application with personal, financial, and scheme-specific details.
Some banks allow online document upload; others require a branch visit.

The bank verifies your information — income documents, ID proof, business registration (for MSME loans), admission letter (for education loans), or land records (for agriculture loans). This takes 3-7 days for small loans (Mudra, SVANidhi) and 15-30 days for larger ones.
If approved, you receive a sanction letter with loan amount, interest rate, tenure, and EMI. Sign the loan agreement and any collateral documentation.
Funds are disbursed to your bank account within 5-10 days for small loans and 2-3 weeks for larger amounts.

You can track application status through the bank's portal. If rejected, Jan Samarth lets you apply to another lender for the same scheme — rejection by one bank doesn't disqualify you from the scheme itself.

how_to_apply.title: Visit jansamarth.in
how_to_apply.description: Open the portal on your phone or computer. No registration needed to browse. Available in Hindi and English.

how_to_apply.title: Find your scheme
how_to_apply.description: Use 'Find Your Scheme' or browse by category — education, business, agriculture, livelihood, housing, skill, startup. Read eligibility, loan amount, and interest rate details for each.

how_to_apply.title: Compare lenders
how_to_apply.description: The portal lists 125+ banks and NBFCs for each scheme. Compare interest rates and processing times. Pick a lender where you already have an account (faster verification) or one near you.

how_to_apply.title: Click Apply and fill application
how_to_apply.description: You'll be redirected to the bank's website. Fill personal details, financial information, and upload required documents — Aadhaar, PAN, income proof, and scheme-specific papers.

how_to_apply.title: Bank verifies and disburses
how_to_apply.description: The bank verifies your application (3-30 days depending on loan type). You'll receive SMS/email updates. Once approved, sign the agreement and funds are disbursed to your account.

important_alert.text: Jan Samarth is completely FREE. No agent, broker, or middleman can charge you for applying through this portal. Many fraudsters in local markets claim they can fast-track loan approvals or guarantee scheme benefits for a fee — they cannot. Apply only through jansamarth.in or directly at a bank branch. Report fraud to the Ministry of Finance or your nearest bank.

important_dates.label: Processing time
important_dates.value: 3-30 days (depends on scheme and loan amount)

faqs.question: Do I need to register on Jan Samarth to browse schemes?
faqs.answer: No. You can explore all 15+ schemes, eligibility criteria, interest rates, and lender options without any registration. Registration is only needed if you want to save preferences. For the actual loan application, you apply through the partner bank's portal.

faqs.question: Is Jan Samarth a bank or loan provider?
faqs.answer: No. Jan Samarth is a GATEWAY — it connects you to government schemes and partner banks. The portal itself doesn't sanction or disburse loans. Your loan comes from the bank you choose (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, etc.), not from Jan Samarth.

faqs.question: Which scheme is best for starting a small business?
faqs.answer: Depends on your business size. PM Mudra is ideal for microenterprises — up to ₹10 lakh, no collateral, 6.5-10% interest. Stand Up India suits women and SC/ST entrepreneurs needing ₹10L-₹1Cr. PMEGP is for first-time entrepreneurs. CGTMSE offers collateral-free loans up to ₹2 crore with government guarantee. Compare all on Jan Samarth.

faqs.question: What documents do I need?
faqs.answer: Basic: Aadhaar, PAN, address proof, bank account. Beyond that, it depends on the scheme — education loans need admission proof and fee structure; business loans need registration and financial statements; agriculture loans need land documents; housing loans need property papers. Jan Samarth lists exact documents for each scheme.

faqs.question: How long does loan approval take?
faqs.answer: Mudra and SVANidhi (small, no collateral): 3-7 days. Education loans: 15-30 days. Housing loans (with property valuation): 30-45 days. Agriculture loans: 20-40 days. The portal shows expected processing time for each lender.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I have a low CIBIL score?
faqs.answer: Some government schemes (Mudra, SVANidhi) are more lenient on credit scores because government guarantee reduces bank risk. However, a CIBIL score below 650 may still cause rejection for larger loans. Check your score first (free annually from cibil.com) and apply to schemes that match your credit profile.

faqs.question: If one bank rejects my application, can I try another?
faqs.answer: Yes. Rejection by one bank doesn't disqualify you from the scheme. You can apply to another participating bank through Jan Samarth. Different banks have different risk appetites — smaller banks and NBFCs sometimes approve applications that larger banks reject.

faqs.question: I don't have collateral. Which schemes work for me?
faqs.answer: Several schemes are collateral-free: PM Mudra (up to ₹10L), PM SVANidhi (up to ₹50K), Education Loans (up to ₹4L), and CGTMSE-backed MSME loans (up to ₹2Cr with government guarantee covering 85%). Check scheme details on Jan Samarth for collateral requirements.

ministry: Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance
